text: All floor openings are covered or guarded
detail: Check that any holes, gaps or openings in floors are covered with fixed boards or protected by barriers. Uncovered openings are a critical fall risk.

text: Emergency exits are clearly marked and unobstructed
detail: Verify all emergency exit signs are lit or clearly visible, doors open outwards freely and no materials are blocking the path.

text: Workers are wearing hard hats in active construction zones
detail: Check all workers in areas with overhead work or falling object risk are wearing EN-397 certified hard hats. Remind site manager if non-compliant.

text: High-visibility vests worn by all workers on site
detail: All workers must wear EN ISO 20471 Class 2 or higher hi-vis vests. Particularly important near vehicle movement areas.

text: Safety footwear worn by all workers
detail: Workers must wear EN ISO 20345 certified safety boots (steel toe cap minimum). Check especially in areas with heavy materials.

text: Fire extinguishers present and within inspection date
detail: Verify at least one extinguisher per 200m2 or per floor. Check the inspection tag - must be within the last 12 months. Correct type for site (ABC powder recommended).
regulation_ref: RD 1627/1997 Annex IV Part A §5 + CTE DB-SI

text: Safety signage visible at all entry points
detail: Check that hazard warning signs, PPE requirement signs and site rules are posted at all entrances. Signs must comply with RD 485/1997 pictogram standards.
regulation_ref: RD 485/1997 + RD 1627/1997 Annex IV Part A §4

text: Site perimeter is secured and clearly marked
detail: Fencing or barriers must enclose the full site perimeter. Gaps or unsecured sections allow unauthorised access.

text: First aid kit is stocked and accessible
detail: Check kit is mounted visibly within expiry dates and contains: bandages antiseptic gloves eye wash emergency contact list. Note location on report.

text: Emergency contact numbers posted on site
detail: A visible sign near site entrance must show: emergency services (112) site manager mobile nearest hospital address and local Labour Inspectorate number.

text: Scaffolding has guardrails on all open sides above 2m
detail: All scaffold platforms above 2 metres must have: main rail at min. 90cm intermediate rail and toe board at least 15cm high. Inspect fixing points for stability.

text: Scaffolding anchor points are secure and undamaged
detail: Check wall ties and anchor bolts are not corroded bent or missing. Each anchor must be load-rated for the scaffold weight. Document any defects found.

text: Scaffolding boards are fully planked with no gaps
detail: No gaps larger than 25mm between boards. Boards must overhang supports by 150-300mm. Check for warped cracked or slippery boards.

text: Electrical panels are locked and clearly labelled
detail: All distribution boards must be locked when unattended. Labels must show voltage circuit IDs and isolation procedure. Check for exposed wiring at entries.
regulation_ref: RD 1627/1997 Annex IV Part A §3 + ITC-BT-17

text: Temporary electrical cables are routed safely overhead or in conduit
detail: No cables trailing across walkways or vehicle routes. Overhead cables must be at min. 2.5m height. All joints must be in IP44 rated enclosures.

text: Exposed wiring or open junction boxes are made safe
detail: Any unfinished electrical work must be isolated at the distribution board and taped with hazard tape. Log location and alert site electrician.

text: Chemical storage area is ventilated and labelled with hazard symbols
detail: Solvents adhesives and other chemicals must be stored in a dedicated ventilated area with GHS hazard labels. Check MSDS sheets are accessible on site.
regulation_ref: RD 374/2001 + RD 1627/1997 Annex IV Part A §7

text: Hazardous waste is segregated and in sealed containers
detail: Chemical waste asbestos fragments and contaminated materials must be in clearly labelled sealed containers. Check waste manifest is present for removal.

text: Machinery guards are in place on all powered tools
detail: Angle grinders circular saws and similar tools must have blade/disc guards fitted. Check emergency stop buttons are accessible and functional.

text: Crane and hoist load limits are posted and observed
detail: Check load rating plate is visible on crane and hoist. No improvised lifting. Slings and shackles must be colour-coded for current inspection period.

text: Vehicle and pedestrian routes are separated on site
detail: Temporary road markings or barriers must separate forklift/vehicle routes from foot traffic. Crossing points must have stop signs or a banksman.

text: Excavations are shored fenced and signed
detail: Any excavation deeper than 1.2m must have shoring or battered sides. Edge barriers must be at least 1m from the excavation edge. Warning signs present.

text: Construction zone is fully fenced and separated from occupied areas
detail: Hoardings must be minimum 2m high and continuous. No gaps adjacent to occupied offices or retail. Dust suppression measures in place if applicable.

text: Noise levels monitored if work is near occupied offices
detail: If construction noise is audible in occupied spaces check decibel readings are within permitted hours (Mon-Fri 8:00-21:00 per local ordinance). Log readings.

text: Handrails on all permanent and temporary staircases
detail: All stairs with more than 3 steps must have at least one handrail. Temporary staircases must have rails on both sides if width exceeds 1.2m.

text: Children's play areas adjacent to site are fully fenced
detail: Any school play area sports court or outdoor space adjacent to the construction zone must be enclosed by minimum 2m solid hoarding. No gaps.
regulation_ref: RD 1627/1997 Annex IV Part A §11 + Art.4

text: Construction vehicles have no access to areas used by students
detail: Vehicle delivery routes must not cross any area accessible to students during school hours. A separate locked gate for deliveries is required.

text: Dust and debris suppression active near occupied classrooms
detail: Water spraying or dust screens must be used during any cutting or drilling within 20m of occupied classrooms. Check filters on ventilation intakes.

text: No percussive work during school hours without written agreement
detail: No breaking pile driving or jackhammering during school exam periods or core teaching hours unless agreed with school management in writing.

text: Hazardous materials stored away from any student-accessible area
detail: Chemical stores fuel tanks and waste skips must be located at least 10m from any boundary students could access. Locks must be present at all times.

text: Emergency plan accounts for school occupants
detail: Site emergency plan must include evacuation coordination with school. Verify site manager has school emergency contact and evacuation routes avoid school exits.
regulation_ref: RD 1627/1997 Art.9 + Annex IV Part A §4

text: Construction warning signs posted at school entrances
detail: Clear warning signs must be at all school entrance points visible to parents students and staff.

text: Safety induction records available for all workers
detail: Site manager must hold a register showing each worker received a safety induction. Check date name and signature. Must be within last 12 months.

text: Welfare facilities (toilets and rest area) are available for workers
detail: Minimum: 1 toilet per 15 workers 1 washbasin per 10 workers lockable area for personal items covered rest space with seating.

text: Drinking water is available on site
detail: Potable water must be accessible within 50m of any active work area. Bottled water acceptable. Label tap water clearly if non-potable.

text: Safety and health plan is on site and up to date
detail: The approved Plan de Seguridad y Salud must be physically present on site. Check it reflects current phase of work and has coordinator signature.

text: Incident logbook (Libro de Incidencias) is present and accessible
detail: The official logbook must be on site held by the safety coordinator or site manager. Must be accessible to all entitled parties.

text: Overhead protection on pedestrian routes below working areas
detail: Covered walkways or solid overhead protection must be in place on any footpath directly below any work at height activity.

text: Work at height uses correct fall protection
detail: Workers above 2m must use: platform with guardrails or harness with anchor point. Check harness condition - no fraying clips lock correctly. No improvised platforms.

text: Adjacent residential units protected from dust and debris
detail: Temporary screens or sheeting must be erected on facades adjacent to occupied residential units. Notify residents in advance of noisy or dusty phases.

text: Structural modifications to occupied residential building are supervised
detail: Any work affecting load-bearing walls floors or structural frame must be carried out under direct supervision of a competent structural engineer.

text: Construction zone is separated from occupied residential areas at all times
detail: A clear boundary must exist between construction zone and occupied areas at all times. Connecting doors must be locked on the construction side.

text: Utility isolations (gas water electric) are documented before structural work
detail: Written confirmation of isolation from the relevant utility provider must be on site. Check date of isolation matches current work phase.

text: Waste materials removed from residential areas daily
detail: Rubble packaging and waste must not accumulate in corridors stairwells or any shared residential space. Removal at minimum daily.
regulation_ref: RD 1627/1997 Annex IV Part A §10 + Art.10

text: Adequate lighting in all active work areas and circulation routes
detail: Minimum 50 lux for general circulation 200 lux for active work areas. Check all temporary lighting is mounted securely and working.
